name: Shabath ibn Ribi
title: Many-Colored Hypocrite
shortDescription: One of Kufa's tribal leaders who initially wrote an invitation letter to Imam Hussain (AS), but in Karbala became the infantry commander of Yazid's army.
fullStory:
Shabath ibn Rib'i is a highly hypocritical, fickle, and worldly figure in Islamic history. He apostatized and claimed prophethood during the Prophet's (PBUH) era, rebelled during Uthman's time, stood with Amir al-Mu'minin at Siffin, then joined the Khawarij, and later grew close to Muawiya's court.

When Imam Hussain (AS) was in Mecca, Shabath was one of Kufa's elders who wrote a passionate letter inviting the Imam. But as soon as Ubaydullah ibn Ziyad entered Kufa with money and threats, Shabath changed colors.

To protect his position and wealth, he paid allegiance to Ibn Ziyad and came to Karbala. On Ashura, he commanded the infantry of Umar ibn Sa'd's army.

After Ashura, Shabath rebuilt his mosque in Kufa in thanksgiving for Imam Hussain's (AS) killing! He later joined Mukhtar's uprising to save his life and eventually died a natural death in old age, leaving behind a dark record of hypocrisy.
quoteArabic: يا شَبَثَ بْنَ رِبْعِيٍّ... أَلَمْ تَكْتُبُوا إِلَيَّ أَنْ قَدْ أَيْنَعَتِ الثِّمارُ...؟
quote: (Imam Hussain addressing him on Ashura:) O Shabath ibn Rib'i! Did you not write to me that the fruits have ripened and we await you?
